h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue placing cards correctly and eventually accept the tutor's explanation of the two-number split.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The decimal belongs here at the bottom. The 6 stays at the top; its job is atomic number, the number that identifies carbon.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly restores the card and names the job of 6, performing the requested placement task, but the same action is repeated in L3 and L4 without any new device or criterion being applied.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The split is clear. The top number lets the tile say which element: 6, C, carbon. The bottom decimal answers a second lookup job: the mass entry for that same element. Put 12.01 back and read the two number jobs separately.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 restates the same explanation and repeats the same placement instruction without stock-taking contrast or a new public mechanism, so neither part A nor part B of the reversal criterion is met.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The split is clear. The top number lets the tile say which element: 6, C, carbon. The bottom decimal answers a second lookup job: the mass entry for that same element. Put 12.01 back and read the two number jobs separately.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's response to the repeated blockage is a renamed restatement of the prior route with no new representation, role, evidence standard, or constraint that would give the learner different work to do.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.
